reddit_title: Hit by a semi truck in Irving, TX — here's what you need to know before you talk to anyone
reddit_body:
Semi truck accidents in Irving are more legally complicated than most people expect. Because commercial trucks are regulated by the FMCSA, there are federal rules around driver hours, vehicle maintenance, and cargo loading that can all become part of your claim. More importantly, the trucking company's legal team often begins building their defense immediately after a crash — so timing on your end matters a lot.

One of the most overlooked issues is evidence preservation. Black box data, electronic logging device records, and dashcam footage can be legally overwritten or destroyed within a short window after an accident. Sending a spoliation letter through an attorney early in the process can prevent that. You should also avoid giving recorded statements to any insurance adjuster — yours or theirs — before speaking with a lawyer who handles truck cases specifically.

If you are trying to find the right attorney and do not know where to start, a free matching service like wreckmatch.com can connect you with lawyers experienced in commercial truck litigation in Texas. No cost, no pressure — just a faster way to find someone who actually knows this area of law.
